people.text: Victor wants an app to organise school assignments. He needs reminders before deadlines and wants to attach photos of notes from class. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

Leah wants to sleep better. She would like relaxing sounds and simple advice, but she does not want an app that tracks personal health data. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

Omar wants to learn new words in English. He needs short daily practice and would like the app to work without internet on the train. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

Kate wants an app for sharing family shopping lists. Several people must be able to edit the same list, and she wants notifications when items are added. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

Felix wants to start running. He needs a beginner plan and spoken instructions during exercise, but he does not care about sharing results online. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

texts.text: StudyTrack lets students create assignment lists, set deadline reminders and attach photos or documents to each task. It also has a calendar view for exams. The app is designed for school and college work rather than office projects. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

SleepSound offers relaxing music, rain sounds and short bedtime tips. It does not collect heart rate, sleep movement or other health data. Users can set a timer so the sounds stop automatically after they fall asleep. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

WordSteps gives five-minute English vocabulary exercises every day. Lessons can be downloaded for offline use, which is helpful on trains or buses. The app reviews difficult words automatically and includes pronunciation examples. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

FamilyBasket allows several users to edit one shopping list in real time. It sends notifications when someone adds or removes an item. Lists can be organised by shop section, and changes appear immediately on everyone’s phone. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

RunStart provides a gentle eight-week plan for new runners. During each session, a voice tells users when to walk, run or rest. The app records progress privately, and social sharing is optional. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

HealthNight analyses sleep patterns using movement and heart-rate data. It gives detailed charts and advice, but users must allow personal health tracking. There are relaxing sounds, although data collection is central to the app. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

WordWorld is a large online English dictionary with games and videos. It has many useful examples, but most activities require an internet connection. There is no structured short daily practice programme. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

RunnerShare is aimed at experienced runners who enjoy competing online. It posts results to leaderboards and connects with social networks. There are training plans, but beginners may find them too difficult. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

answers.explanation: Victor needs school assignment reminders and note photos; A matches.
Leah wants relaxing sleep sounds and advice without health tracking; B fits.
Omar needs short English vocabulary practice offline; C matches.
Kate needs shared editable shopping lists with notifications; D fits.
Felix needs a beginner running plan with spoken instructions; E matches.
